title: Men's universal right to vote
subtitle: This does not consider elections not being held, informal restrictions, or legal restrictions based on age, criminal conviction, disability, and local residency.
caption:
Men's universal right to vote

This does not consider elections not being held, informal restrictions, or legal restrictions based on age, criminal conviction, disability, and local residency.

This choropleth world map displays the status of men's universal right to vote in 2025 across global territories. The legend defines three categories: "No data," represented by a hatched diagonal pattern; "No," represented by an orange color; and "Yes," represented by a light blue color. The vast majority of the world's landmass, including North America, South America, Europe, Asia, Australia, and most of Africa, is colored in light blue, indicating that men have the universal right to vote in these regions. In contrast, only two countries in the Middle East and East Africa appear in orange, signifying a lack of this right: Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates. Greenland is the only territory clearly marked with the hatched diagonal pattern, indicating that no data is available for this region. The visual distribution shows a near-global consensus on men's voting rights, with exceptions limited to the Arabian Peninsula and a data gap in the high north. The subtitle clarifies that this classification does not account for elections not being held, informal restrictions, or legal restrictions based on age, criminal conviction, disability, and local residency.
